title: Spear & Jackson SJ-BSS24 24oz Ball Pein Hammer
features: Ball Pein Hammers, also known as the Machinist Hammer, are designed with one flat face and one ball face
The flat side is used for striking punches and chisels
The rounded head is used for rounding off edges of metal pins and fasteners, such as rivets. It may also be used for shaping materials and flattening or removing dents from sheet metals
Solid forged from one piece of carbon steel for strength and durability, with a polished striking face with chamfered edges to help avoid chipping
Ergonomic bi-material handles with soft-feel shock-reducing grips for secure and comfortable handling
description: From the manufacturer Spear & Jackson Spear & Jackson has been selling hand, garden, contractors, agricultural and landscaping tools since 1760. The company was founded in the City of Sheffield, which is famous for the production of steel, cutlery and hand tools and continues to be our home over 250 years later. Our Solid Steel Hammers Our collection of Solid Steel hammers are solid-forged from one piece of carbon steel, making them super strong and durable. Soft-feel grips allow for comfortable and ergonomic use, and help to reduce shock impact. The polished striking faces have chamfered edges to avoid chipping, keeping these hammers looking at their best! Brick Hammer (22oz) Claw Hammer (15oz) Club Hammer (3lb) Ball Pein Hammers (16, 24, 32oz) Double Faced Sledge Hammer (10lb) Our Range of Solid Steel Hammers Brick Hammer (22oz) Ideal for splitting, trimming, cleaning and setting bricks, stone or masonry Claw Hammer (15oz) Primarily used in carpentry for driving nails into or pulling nails from wood Club Hammer (3lb) Ideal for delivering short, heavy blows and are used with cold chisels and brick bolsters for light demolition work and for cutting rocks or metal Ball Pein Hammers (16, 24, 32oz) Ball Pein Hammers, also known as the Machinist Hammer, are designed with one flat face and one ball face Double Faced Sledge Hammer (10lb) Required for breaking through drywall, masonry and breaking up rocks and concrete. Ideal for heavy duty and demoltion work. Large flat metal head attached to a long handle allowing momentum gain whilst swinging, delivering much greater force than a standard hammer.
